title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Quantity Clinic: Solver Modeling
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int solver modeling increment in an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Solver Modeling evidence
steps.instruction: Calculate the determinant of [[4, 1], [2, 7]]. Use the result to decide Solver Modeling for an explainable constrained optimization model.
steps.why: The Solver Modeling result for an explainable constrained optimization model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (4 × 7) − (1 × 2). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
